name: Mock Data Generator
description: Generates N mock records for a given backend_id and table. Seeds Python's random module and Faker per-field (using field index offset from base seed) to ensure deterministic output independent of field order changes. Field type mapping: uuid→uuid4, varchar→random string of appropriate length, bigint→random int, boolean→random bool, timestamptz→random datetime. Classification-aware generation: PII fields use Faker for realistic fakes (email, name, address), FINANCIAL fields use plausible monetary fakes. encrypted_at_rest and tokenized fields always produce token-shaped values (tok_{base64url_24chars}), never raw plaintext. Canary mode (--purpose canary) generates fingerprinted values matching ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8} pattern shaped to field type (email gets @canary.invalid suffix, payment_token gets tok_ prefix). Canary registration with Arbiter attempted via httpx if arbiter_api is configured; skipped with warning if not. Seed from ledger.yaml mock.seed, overridable via --seed CLI flag.

types.name: FieldClassification
types.description: Classification tier for a schema field, determining which generator strategy is used.

types.name: MockPurpose
types.description: The purpose of mock data generation, controlling value shaping strategy.

types.name: ViolationSeverity
types.description: Severity level for mock generation violations.

types.name: FieldSpec

fields.name: field_name
fields.description: Column name in the target table.

fields.name: sql_type
fields.description: Raw SQL type string, e.g. 'varchar(255)', 'bigint', 'uuid', 'boolean', 'timestamptz'.

fields.name: max_length
fields.description: Maximum character length parsed from varchar(N). None for non-varchar types.

validators.error_message: max_length must be between 1 and 65535 if specified.

fields.name: classification
fields.description: Classification tier of the field. None treated as PUBLIC for generation purposes.

fields.name: encrypted_at_rest
fields.description: If true, generated value is always a token-shaped string (tok_{base64url_24chars}).

fields.name: tokenized
fields.description: If true, generated value is always a token-shaped string (tok_{base64url_24chars}).

fields.name: nullable
fields.description: If true, there is a configurable probability (default 10%) the generated value is None.

types.description: Specification of a single table field for mock generation. sql_type is the raw SQL type string (e.g. 'varchar(255)', 'bigint', 'timestamptz'). max_length is parsed from varchar(N) if present; None otherwise.

types.name: MockGenerationRequest

fields.name: backend_id
fields.description: Identifier of the backend system owning the table.

fields.name: table_name
fields.description: Name of the table to generate mock records for.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: List of field specifications for the table. Must contain at least one field.

validators.error_message: At least one field must be specified.

fields.name: row_count
fields.description: Number of mock records to generate.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Override seed for deterministic generation. If None, uses mock.seed from ledger.yaml config.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: Purpose of generation: 'test' for standard mock data, 'canary' for fingerprinted canary values.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: Data tier label for canary mode. Required when purpose is 'canary'. Used in canary pattern: ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8}.

validators.expression: purpose != 'canary' or (value is not None and len(value) >= 1)
validators.error_message: tier is required and must be non-empty when purpose is 'canary'.

fields.name: arbiter_api
fields.description: Base URL for Arbiter API. If provided and purpose is 'canary', canary registration is attempted via httpx.

validators.error_message: arbiter_api must be a valid HTTP(S) URL.

fields.name: null_probability
fields.description: Probability that a nullable field produces None for a given row.

types.description: Request model for mock data generation. Validated by Pydantic with cross-field model_validator enforcing that tier is required when purpose is 'canary'.

types.name: MockViolation

fields.name: field_name
fields.description: Name of the field that triggered the violation. May be empty for request-level violations.

fields.name: error_type
fields.description: Machine-readable error category, e.g. 'unsupported_type', 'arbiter_unreachable', 'validation_error'.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the violation.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity level: 'error' for generation failures, 'warning' for fallbacks and non-fatal issues.

types.description: A single violation or warning encountered during mock generation.

types.name: MockGenerationResult

fields.name: records
fields.description: Generated mock records. Each dict maps field_name to generated value.

fields.name: seed_used
fields.description: The base seed that was used for generation (from CLI, request, or config).

fields.name: canary_registered
fields.description: True if canary registration succeeded, False if attempted and failed, None if not attempted.

fields.name: warnings
fields.description: Human-readable warning messages (e.g. arbiter skipped, unsupported type fallback).

fields.name: errors
fields.description: Structured list of all violations encountered during generation.

fields.name: row_count
fields.description: Actual number of records generated (should equal request row_count unless errors occurred).

types.description: Result of mock data generation. Contains the generated records, the seed used, canary registration status, and any warnings or errors encountered.

types.name: CanaryRegistrationResult

fields.name: success
fields.description: True if Arbiter accepted the canary registration.

fields.name: arbiter_response_code
fields.description: HTTP status code from Arbiter, if a response was received.

fields.name: registration_id
fields.description: Unique registration ID returned by Arbiter on success.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Error description if registration failed.

types.description: Result of attempting to register canary values with the Arbiter API.

types.name: CanaryValue

fields.name: field_name
fields.description: The field this canary value belongs to.

fields.name: row_index
fields.description: Zero-based row index within the generated batch.

fields.name: raw_fingerprint
fields.description: The raw canary fingerprint string: ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8}.

fields.name: shaped_value
fields.description: The canary value shaped to the field's SQL type (e.g. email with @canary.invalid suffix).

types.description: A single canary fingerprint value for a specific field and row.

types.name: TypeGeneratorEntry

fields.name: sql_type_pattern
fields.description: SQL type name (lowercase, without length modifiers), e.g. 'uuid', 'varchar', 'bigint', 'boolean', 'timestamptz'.

fields.name: generator_fn_name
fields.description: Fully qualified name of the generator function: (Random, FieldSpec, int) -> any.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this generator produces.

types.description: An entry in the TYPE_GENERATORS registry mapping an SQL type to its generator callable.

types.name: ClassificationGeneratorEntry

fields.name: classification
fields.description: The classification tier this generator handles.

fields.name: generator_fn_name
fields.description: Fully qualified name of the generator function: (Faker, FieldSpec, int) -> any.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this generator produces.

types.description: An entry in the CLASSIFICATION_GENERATORS registry mapping a classification tier to its Faker-based generator callable.

types.name: SeedInfo

fields.name: base_seed
fields.description: The base seed from config or CLI override.

fields.name: field_index_offset
fields.description: Zero-based position of this field in the lexicographically sorted list of all field names.

fields.name: field_seed
fields.description: Computed as base_seed + field_index_offset.

fields.name: field_name
fields.description: The field name this seed info belongs to.

types.description: Computed seed information for a specific field, used internally during generation.

functions.name: generate_mock_records
functions.description: Primary entry point. Generates N mock records for the specified table and fields according to the MockGenerationRequest. Applies the precedence chain: canary > tokenized/encrypted > classification > type > fallback. Returns a MockGenerationResult with records, seed used, canary registration status, warnings, and aggregated errors. Never raises exceptions for generation issues; all problems are captured in the result.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: Fully validated mock generation request specifying backend, table, fields, row count, seed, purpose, and optional arbiter API.

error_cases.name: invalid_request
error_cases.condition: MockGenerationRequest fails Pydantic validation (e.g. row_count < 1, missing tier for canary).

error_data.detail: Aggregated list of all Pydantic validation errors as MockViolation entries.

error_cases.name: no_seed_available
error_cases.condition: request.seed is None and ledger.yaml mock.seed is not configured or unreadable.

error_data.detail: No seed available: neither --seed flag nor mock.seed in ledger.yaml is set.

error_cases.name: unsupported_sql_type
error_cases.condition: A field's sql_type does not match any entry in TYPE_GENERATORS and no classification override applies.

error_data.detail: Unsupported SQL type '{sql_type}' for field '{field_name}'; falling back to random string.

error_cases.name: canary_tier_missing
error_cases.condition: purpose is 'canary' but tier is None or empty.

error_data.detail: tier is required when purpose is 'canary'.

error_cases.name: arbiter_connection_failure
error_cases.condition: Canary registration attempted but httpx request to arbiter_api fails (timeout, DNS, HTTP error).

error_data.detail: Arbiter registration failed: {error}. Canary values generated but not registered.

error_cases.name: arbiter_not_configured
error_cases.condition: purpose is 'canary' but arbiter_api is None.

error_data.detail: Arbiter API not configured; canary registration skipped.

error_cases.name: duplicate_field_names
error_cases.condition: Two or more FieldSpec entries have the same field_name.

preconditions: request has been validated by Pydantic (all field-level and model-level validators pass).
request.fields contains at least one FieldSpec.
If request.purpose is 'canary', request.tier is not None and not empty.
postconditions: result.records has length equal to request.row_count unless generation-time errors prevented some rows.
result.seed_used reflects the actual seed used (from request.seed, or config fallback).
Every record in result.records has exactly len(request.fields) keys matching the field names.
All tokenized or encrypted_at_rest field values match pattern tok_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{24}.
All canary field values (when purpose='canary') contain the substring ledger-canary-{tier}.
result.canary_registered is None when purpose is 'test' or arbiter_api is None.
result.canary_registered is True or False when purpose is 'canary' and arbiter_api is not None.
Unsupported SQL types produce at least one MockViolation with severity 'warning' and the field falls back to random string generation.
side_effects: Attempts HTTP POST to Arbiter API when purpose='canary' and arbiter_api is configured.

structured_side_effects.description: Registers canary fingerprint values via httpx POST when purpose='canary' and arbiter_api is configured. Wrapped in try/except; failure produces a warning, never an exception.
Logs warnings for unsupported types, arbiter failures, and missing configuration.

functions.name: compute_field_seeds
functions.description: Computes the deterministic per-field seed info for all fields in a request. Sorts field names lexicographically to assign stable field_index_offsets, then computes field_seed = base_seed + field_index_offset for each field.

inputs.name: field_names
inputs.description: List of field names from the request (need not be sorted).

inputs.name: base_seed
inputs.description: The resolved base seed (from CLI or config).

error_cases.name: empty_field_names
error_cases.condition: field_names list is empty.

error_data.detail: field_names must contain at least one field name.

error_cases.name: duplicate_field_names
error_cases.condition: field_names contains duplicates.

postconditions: Returned list has same length as field_names.
Each SeedInfo.field_index_offset is unique and in range [0, len(field_names)).
SeedInfo entries are sorted by field_name lexicographically.
SeedInfo.field_seed == base_seed + SeedInfo.field_index_offset for every entry.
Output is fully deterministic: same inputs always produce same outputs.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: generate_field_value
functions.description: Generates a single value for a specific field and row index. Applies the precedence chain: canary > tokenized/encrypted > classification > type > fallback. Creates an isolated Random or Faker instance seeded with (field_seed + row_index). Returns the generated value or a fallback string with a MockViolation if the type is unsupported.

inputs.name: field_spec
inputs.description: Specification of the field to generate a value for.

inputs.name: field_seed
inputs.description: Pre-computed per-field seed (base_seed + field_index_offset).

inputs.name: row_index
inputs.description: Zero-based row index within the batch.

inputs.name: purpose
inputs.description: Generation purpose: 'test' or 'canary'.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: Data tier for canary fingerprinting. Required when purpose is 'canary'.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Backend identifier, used for canary hex8 computation.

inputs.name: table_name
inputs.description: Table name, used for canary hex8 computation.

inputs.name: null_probability
inputs.description: Probability of returning None for nullable fields.

error_cases.name: unsupported_type_fallback
error_cases.condition: field_spec.sql_type is not in TYPE_GENERATORS and no classification override applies.

error_data.detail: Falling back to random string for unsupported type.

error_cases.name: canary_without_tier
error_cases.condition: purpose is 'canary' but tier is None.

error_data.detail: tier must be provided for canary generation.

preconditions: If purpose is 'canary', tier is not None and not empty.
postconditions: If field_spec.tokenized or field_spec.encrypted_at_rest is true (and purpose != 'canary'), result matches pattern tok_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{24}.
If purpose is 'canary', result contains the canary fingerprint shaped to the field type.
If field_spec.nullable is true and the seeded random check hits null_probability, result is None.
Same inputs always produce the same output (deterministic).

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function; creates isolated Random/Faker instances internally, no shared state mutation.

functions.name: generate_canary_fingerprint
functions.description: Generates the raw canary fingerprint string for a specific field and row. Computes hex8 as the first 8 hex characters of SHA-256(backend_id + table_name + field_name + str(row_index)). Returns the fingerprint in format: ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8}.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Backend identifier.

inputs.name: table_name
inputs.description: Table name.

inputs.name: field_name
inputs.description: Field name.

inputs.name: row_index
inputs.description: Zero-based row index.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: Data tier label for the canary pattern.

error_cases.name: empty_tier
error_cases.condition: tier is an empty string.

postconditions: Result matches pattern ledger-canary-{tier}-[0-9a-f]{8}.
hex8 portion equals hashlib.sha256((backend_id + table_name + field_name + str(row_index)).encode()).hexdigest()[:8].
Deterministic: same inputs always produce the same output.

functions.name: shape_canary_to_type
functions.description: Shapes a raw canary fingerprint string to match the expected format for a given SQL type and field classification. For example: email fields get '@canary.invalid' suffix, payment_token fields get 'tok_' prefix, uuid fields get a valid UUID structure with embedded fingerprint, varchar fields are truncated to max_length.

inputs.name: raw_fingerprint
inputs.description: Raw canary fingerprint string (ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8}).

inputs.name: field_spec
inputs.description: Field specification to determine shaping rules.

error_cases.name: fingerprint_too_long_for_field
error_cases.condition: Shaped fingerprint exceeds field_spec.max_length after shaping.

error_data.detail: Canary fingerprint truncated to fit max_length constraint.

postconditions: Result contains the raw_fingerprint substring (or a recognizable derivative) for canary detection.
If field_spec.sql_type starts with 'varchar' and field_spec.max_length is set, result length <= max_length.
If field_spec.sql_type is 'uuid', result is a valid UUID-formatted string.
If field_spec.classification is PII and field_name contains 'email', result ends with '@canary.invalid'.
If field_spec.tokenized is true, result starts with 'tok_'.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: generate_token_value
functions.description: Generates a token-shaped value matching pattern tok_{base64url_24chars}. Used for fields marked as tokenized or encrypted_at_rest. Uses the provided seeded Random instance for deterministic base64url character selection.

inputs.name: rng
inputs.description: A seeded random.Random instance for deterministic generation.

preconditions: rng is a random.Random instance that has been properly seeded.
postconditions: Result matches pattern tok_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{24}.
Result length is exactly 28 characters (4 prefix + 24 payload).
Deterministic: same seed produces same token.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function using only the provided Random instance.

functions.name: register_canary_with_arbiter
functions.description: Attempts to register generated canary fingerprint values with the Arbiter API via httpx POST. Wraps the call in try/except to ensure failures never propagate as exceptions. Returns a CanaryRegistrationResult indicating success/failure.

inputs.name: arbiter_api
inputs.description: Base URL of the Arbiter API (e.g. 'https://arbiter.internal:8443').

inputs.name: canary_values
inputs.description: List of canary fingerprint values to register.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: Data tier label.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Backend system identifier.

inputs.name: table_name
inputs.description: Target table name.

error_cases.name: connection_timeout
error_cases.condition: httpx request times out.

error_cases.name: dns_resolution_failure
error_cases.condition: Arbiter hostname cannot be resolved.

error_cases.name: http_error_response
error_cases.condition: Arbiter returns HTTP 4xx or 5xx.

error_cases.name: invalid_response_body
error_cases.condition: Arbiter returns 2xx but response body is not valid JSON or missing registration_id.

preconditions: arbiter_api is a valid HTTP(S) URL.
postconditions: If Arbiter returns HTTP 2xx, result.success is True and result.registration_id is set.
If Arbiter returns non-2xx or connection fails, result.success is False and result.error_message describes the failure.
Function never raises an exception regardless of network conditions.
result.arbiter_response_code is set if any HTTP response was received.

structured_side_effects.description: HTTP POST with JSON payload containing canary values, tier, backend_id, and table_name. Uses httpx with a 10-second timeout.
Logs warning on registration failure with error details.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of canary values serialized

functions.name: resolve_seed
functions.description: Resolves the base seed to use for generation. Checks in order: explicit seed parameter (from CLI --seed or request), then mock.seed from ledger.yaml config. Raises MockGenerationError if neither is available.

inputs.name: explicit_seed
inputs.description: Seed provided via CLI flag or request field. Takes highest priority.

inputs.name: config_seed
inputs.description: Seed from ledger.yaml mock.seed section. Used as fallback.

error_cases.name: no_seed_available
error_cases.condition: Both explicit_seed and config_seed are None.

error_data.detail: No seed available: provide --seed flag or set mock.seed in ledger.yaml.

postconditions: Returns explicit_seed if not None, else config_seed if not None.
Raises MockGenerationError if both are None.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: parse_varchar_length
functions.description: Parses the max_length from a varchar SQL type string. Extracts N from 'varchar(N)' or 'character varying(N)'. Returns None if the type is not a varchar variant or has no length specifier.

inputs.name: sql_type
inputs.description: Raw SQL type string, e.g. 'varchar(255)', 'varchar', 'character varying(100)', 'bigint'.

error_cases.name: invalid_length_value
error_cases.condition: varchar(N) where N is not a valid positive integer (e.g. varchar(-1), varchar(abc)).

postconditions: If sql_type matches 'varchar(N)' or 'character varying(N)', returns N as int.
If sql_type is 'varchar' without length, returns None.
If sql_type is not a varchar variant, returns None.
Returned value, if not None, is >= 1.

functions.name: get_type_generator
functions.description: Looks up the appropriate generator function for a given SQL type from the TYPE_GENERATORS registry. Returns the generator callable if found, or None if the type is not supported (caller should use fallback).

inputs.name: sql_type
inputs.description: Normalized SQL type name (lowercase, without length modifiers), e.g. 'uuid', 'varchar', 'bigint'.

preconditions: sql_type is lowercase and stripped of length modifiers.
postconditions: Returns a callable with signature (Random, FieldSpec, int) -> any if sql_type is in TYPE_GENERATORS.
Returns None if sql_type is not found in TYPE_GENERATORS.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure lookup in the TYPE_GENERATORS dict.

functions.name: get_classification_generator
functions.description: Looks up the appropriate Faker-based generator function for a given field classification from the CLASSIFICATION_GENERATORS registry. Returns the generator callable if found (PII or FINANCIAL), or None for PUBLIC/INTERNAL classifications.

inputs.name: classification
inputs.description: Field classification tier. None is treated as PUBLIC.

postconditions: Returns a callable with signature (Faker, FieldSpec, int) -> any if classification is PII or FINANCIAL.
Returns None if classification is PUBLIC, INTERNAL, or None.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure lookup in the CLASSIFICATION_GENERATORS dict.

functions.name: validate_request
functions.description: Validates a MockGenerationRequest, returning an aggregated list of all violations found. Checks Pydantic field validators, cross-field constraints (tier required for canary), and semantic rules (no duplicate field names, valid SQL types).

inputs.name: raw_input
inputs.description: Raw input dictionary to validate against MockGenerationRequest schema.

error_cases.name: completely_invalid_input
error_cases.condition: Input is not a dict or is missing all required fields.

error_data.detail: All detected violations returned as MockViolation list.

postconditions: Returns an empty list if input is fully valid.
Returns all violations found (not just the first), each as a MockViolation.
Violations include field-level type errors, range violations, and cross-field constraint failures.

invariants: Generator precedence chain is always: canary > tokenized/encrypted > classification > type > fallback. No step may be skipped or reordered.

Per-field seed is computed as base_seed + field_index_offset where field_index_offset is the field's zero-based position in the lexicographically sorted list of field names. This ensures determinism independent of field declaration order.
Per-row seed is computed as field_seed + row_index, giving each (field, row) pair a unique deterministic seed.
Each field gets its own random.Random instance seeded with field_seed; PII fields additionally get their own Faker instance seeded with field_seed.
tokenized or encrypted_at_rest fields always produce values matching pattern tok_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{24}, never raw plaintext, regardless of classification or canary mode.
Canary values always match the pattern ledger-canary-{tier}-[0-9a-f]{8} shaped to the field type (email suffix @canary.invalid, payment_token prefix tok_).
The hex8 portion of canary values is derived as the first 8 hex characters of SHA-256(backend_id + table_name + field_name + str(row_index)).
Unsupported SQL types produce a warning MockViolation and fall back to generating a random string, never raising an exception.
Arbiter registration failures are caught, logged as warnings, and never propagate exceptions to the caller.
All Pydantic validation errors are aggregated and returned as a list of MockViolation entries, not raised individually.
When purpose is 'canary', tier must be provided and non-empty.
Fields with nullable=true have a configurable probability (default 10%) of generating None for that cell.

data_access.rationale: Reads schema metadata (field names, SQL types, classification tiers, encryption/tokenization flags) from the registry to determine how to generate each field. Writes synthetic records across all classification tiers: PUBLIC and INTERNAL fields get type-based random values, PII fields get Faker-generated realistic fakes (emails, names, addresses), FINANCIAL fields get plausible monetary fakes. Canary mode writes fingerprinted values across all tiers. Arbiter registration transmits canary fingerprint values externally.

side_effects.rationale: Registers canary fingerprint values with the Arbiter API via httpx POST when arbiter_api is configured and purpose is canary. Transmits the generated canary values along with tier, backend_id, and table_name for downstream canary detection.
Reads base seed and optional arbiter_api URL from ledger.yaml configuration to initialize deterministic generation and optional canary registration.

authority.rationale: Mock Data Generator is the sole authoritative source for deterministic mock record generation and canary fingerprint value creation within the Ledger system. No other component generates mock or canary data.
